# Design Red-Team Rubric

The 10-point taste test, scored harshly. See `.claude/skills/award-website-os/references/13-awwwards-quality-taste-rubric.md` and `53-design-red-team-rubric.md`.

Threshold for ship: **average ≥ 8.5, no category below 7.5.**

Each category is scored 1–10 with one decimal. Each score must cite a screenshot or specific section. "Looks good" is not a score; it's an abdication.

Lowest category: <FILL IN — must be ≥ 7.5 to ship>

## Anti-genericity check (binary, must pass)

Per `.claude/skills/award-website-os/references/14-ai-anti-genericity-protocol.md`:

- [ ] Site has typography that doesn't look like a popular UI library default.
- [ ] Site has a color treatment that's not a gradient blob.
- [ ] Site has at least one composition that isn't centered stacked sections.
- [ ] Site has a signature moment that isn't "every section fades in".
- [ ] Copy could only be this business (no generic SaaS sentences).
- [ ] Mobile composition isn't a desktop squeeze.

If any box is unchecked, the site is generic. Loop.
